description: Capability map for Ship's git-native package registry and resolver. Each capability has acceptance criteria, priority (p0=must/p1=should/p2=could), and milestone (v0.1/v0.2/beyond).

manifest_format.description: ship.toml inside .ship/ defines module identity, dependencies, exports, and provider targets
acceptance_criteria: ship.toml is discovered at .ship/ship.toml (not repo root)
[module] section: name (namespaced path), version (semver), description, license
[dependencies] section: package path → version constraint (semver, branch, or commit SHA)
[exports] section: explicit skills and agents that are publicly resolvable; everything else private
[compile] section: providers list, per-provider overrides
[resolver] section: sumdb and proxy URLs with defaults
Presence of ship.toml makes the directory a valid ship package
TOML only — no YAML or JSON alternates accepted by tooling

lockfile_format.description: ship.lock captures the fully-resolved dependency graph with content hashes for reproducible installs
acceptance_criteria: ship.lock is committed alongside ship.toml
Each entry: dep path, resolved version, git commit SHA, content hash (sha256)
ship install with existing lockfile is deterministic — identical output every time
ship install without lockfile resolves from constraints and writes lockfile
Lockfile format is human-readable TOML
Modifying ship.toml deps without running ship update produces a lockfile mismatch error

skill_format.description: Skills conform to the agentskills.io specification. Ship is a conforming implementation, not the spec owner.
acceptance_criteria: A skill is a directory with SKILL.md at minimum
SKILL.md has YAML frontmatter + markdown body
Required frontmatter: name (matches directory name, lowercase+hyphens, max 64 chars), description (max 1024 chars)
Optional frontmatter: license, compatibility, metadata (k/v map), allowed-tools (space-delimited)
allowed-tools is the permission declaration surface for the skill
Optional directories: scripts/, references/, assets/
Progressive disclosure: name+description loaded at startup, body on activation, resources on demand
ship validate passes on a spec-conformant skill
skills-ref validate (agentskills reference impl) passes on Ship-authored skills

agent_format.description: An agent is Ship's composition format: rules + skills + permissions + MCP + provider config. Formalizes the existing .ship/agents/profiles/ structure.
acceptance_criteria: Agent defined as a TOML file under .ship/agents/
rules: list of local markdown paths or dependency references (dep-path/rules/file.md)
skills: list of local skill directory paths or dependency references (dep-path/skills/skill-name)
permissions: explicit grant list — must cover all required allowed-tools from referenced skills
mcp: server declarations (command, args, env)
providers: list of compile targets
providers.<name>: per-provider overrides (model, context_window, etc.)
An agent with only rules and no skills is valid
An agent with only skills and no rules is valid
'Profile' and 'Mode' are not used as user-facing type names

resolver.description: Resolves the dependency graph from ship.toml constraints to exact versions, writes ship.lock
acceptance_criteria: Resolves semver tag constraints (^1.0.0, ~1.2.0, =1.2.3)
Resolves branch pins (main, develop) — locked to current HEAD commit at resolve time
Resolves exact commit SHAs
Diamond dependency: same package required at compatible versions → unified resolution
Diamond dependency: incompatible versions → hard error with explanation of conflict path
Transitive dependencies are resolved and locked (not just direct deps)
Resolution is deterministic given the same constraints and same external state
ship update [dep] re-resolves a specific dep and updates lockfile
ship update re-resolves all deps

content_addressed_cache.description: ~/.ship/cache/ stores package content by SHA256 hash. pnpm model: one entry per unique content, zero per-project duplication.
acceptance_criteria: Cache root: ~/.ship/cache/objects/<sha256>/
Cache index: ~/.ship/cache/index/<dep-path>@<version> → <sha256>
Two packages with identical content share one cache entry
ship install with warm cache and unchanged lockfile makes zero network requests
Cache is global across all projects on the machine
ship cache clean removes entries not referenced by any project lockfile
Cache corruption (hash mismatch) causes re-fetch, not silent use of bad content

permissions_model.description: Strict, explicit permission grants. Compiler enforces. No transitive escalation.
acceptance_criteria: A skill's allowed-tools declares what it requires
Consuming agent must list each required tool in its permissions section
Compiler refuses compilation if a required permission is not explicitly granted
Transitive: skill A depends on skill B (which has allowed-tools: Bash). Skill A does NOT inherit Bash unless it also grants it.
ship audit prints the full permission tree: which agent grants what, sourced from which skill
ship audit --violations prints only unganted required permissions
Permissions are carried through to provider-native formats that support them (e.g. allowed-tools in Claude's agent config)

public_private_exports.description: Everything in .ship/ is private by default. Only what is explicitly listed in [exports] is resolvable as a dependency.
acceptance_criteria: No exports section = package has no public surface (valid for consume-only projects)
[exports] skills = list of skill directory paths relative to .ship/
[exports] agents = list of agent TOML paths relative to .ship/
A dep reference to a non-exported path fails at resolve time with a clear error
ship publish validates that all exported paths exist and are valid before tagging

compilation.description: ship install resolves + compiles to provider-native targets. No intermediate .agents/ output.
acceptance_criteria: ship install = resolve (or use lockfile) + fetch to cache + compile to all declared providers
Compilation merges: local .ship/ definitions + resolved dependencies
Output: provider-native files only (CLAUDE.md, .claude/agents/, .cursor/rules/, agents.json, etc.)
.agents/ is written only when a provider target explicitly expects it
No ship-specific files in the output — output is indistinguishable from hand-authored provider config
Compilation is deterministic: same lockfile + same local files = identical output
ship compile --dry-run prints what would be written without writing

cli_core.description: Core CLI commands for the package lifecycle
acceptance_criteria: ship install — resolve lockfile, fetch missing packages, compile to provider targets
ship add <git-path>[@version] — add dependency, resolve, update lockfile, compile
ship update [dep] — re-resolve dep(s), update lockfile, compile
ship publish — validate exports, create git tag, push
ship audit — print permission tree across all dependencies
ship validate — validate .ship/ structure and all exports against their respective specs
ship cache clean — prune unreferenced cache entries
All commands exit non-zero on error with actionable message
All commands are idempotent where semantically appropriate

sum_database.description: Append-only, publicly auditable content hash registry at sum.ship.dev. The cryptographic anchor of the ecosystem.
sum_database.notes: Must be operational before public launch of resolver. A resolver without sum DB verification is incomplete.
acceptance_criteria: sum.ship.dev records: package-path@version → sha256 of content tree
Records are immutable once written — no update, no delete
Public HTTP API: GET /lookup/<path>@<version> returns hash + timestamp
Public HTTP API: GET /tile/* implements transparency log tile protocol
Resolver verifies every installed package against sum DB by default
GONOSUMCHECK equivalent: SHIP_GONOSUMCHECK pattern for internal/private packages
Tampered package (hash mismatch vs sum DB) causes hard install failure
Log is publicly auditable — third parties can monitor for unexpected entries

proxy_protocol.description: proxy.ship.dev caches git package content. Resolvers default to proxy. Private proxy = paid tier.
acceptance_criteria: HTTP API: GET /{path}/@v/list — list available versions
HTTP API: GET /{path}/@v/{version}.info — version metadata (commit, timestamp)
HTTP API: GET /{path}/@v/{version}.zip — package content as zip
Resolver default: proxy.ship.dev,direct (proxy first, fall back to git)
Packages cached on first request — deleted git repos don't break installs
Private proxy endpoint for paid orgs with access-controlled packages
SHIP_PROXY env var overrides proxy config
SHIP_PROXY=direct bypasses proxy entirely (fully offline/air-gapped mode)

registry_index.description: Discovery and search at registry.ship.dev. No storage — indexes git repos.
acceptance_criteria: Search by name, description, skill type, provider compatibility
Package page: readme, version history, dependency graph, permission summary
Crawls public git repos containing ship.toml
Does not store package content — links to git + proxy
Download counts (from proxy logs)
Verified publisher badge for registered publishers
API: GET /search?q= returns package list with metadata

namespace_registration.description: Registered short namespaces prevent dependency confusion. github.com/ paths are unregistered and open.
acceptance_criteria: github.com/owner/repo path resolution requires no registration — always resolves via git
Short names (org/package without host) require registration at ship.dev
Org scope registration: org registers a prefix, all packages under it require that org's auth to publish
Dependency confusion protection: private package name cannot be shadowed by a public package with the same short name
Typosquatting detection: publish is flagged if new name is within edit distance 2 of an existing verified publisher name
Namespace transfer requires both parties to confirm

publisher_identity.description: Package signing, verified publisher badges, and key registration.
acceptance_criteria: Publishers register a signing key with ship.dev
ship publish signs the release with the registered key
Signature stored in sum DB alongside content hash
Verified publisher badge on registry.ship.dev for registered publishers
Resolver can require signed packages: [resolver] require_signed = true

private_registry.description: Org-scoped private proxy with access control. Paid tier.
acceptance_criteria: Orgs can configure proxy.orgname.ship.dev scoped to their packages
Private packages backed by private git repos — access controlled by ship.dev auth
Resolver config: proxy = 'proxy.myco.ship.dev,proxy.ship.dev,direct'
Private packages do not appear in public registry index
Package access auditable: who fetched what, when

github_app_integration.description: GitHub App enables automated dep updates, PR creation on publish, and webhook-driven recompilation.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub App receives push webhooks on repos that depend on changed packages
Automated PR: when a dep releases a new version, open a PR updating ship.lock
ship publish can create a GitHub release via the App (alternative to manual tagging)
Webhook: push to main → re-run ship install → commit updated provider targets if changed
Security: webhook payloads verified with HMAC

studio_publish_flow.description: Ship Studio supports drafting agents/skills and publishing them as versioned packages.
acceptance_criteria: Studio draft (D1-backed) can be promoted to a git-published package
Version bump UI: suggest next semver, write to ship.toml, commit, tag, push
Registry page for the published package linked from Studio
Studio can install packages from the registry into the current project

slsa_provenance.description: Build attestations linking published packages to their source commit and build environment.
acceptance_criteria: Verified publishers can attest builds with SLSA level 2 provenance
Provenance links: package@version → source commit → build environment
Attestations stored in sum DB alongside content hash
ship verify <package> displays provenance chain

workflows.description: Workflow type: composition of agents with execution logic. Ship Pro feature.
workflows.notes: Deliberately out of scope for v0.1 and v0.2. Workflows require the Ship runtime substrate to be useful. Premature workflow packaging without runtime creates dead artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: workflow.toml type: references agents, defines execution graph
ship publish supports workflow type
Workflow packages resolvable as dependencies
Workflow execution requires Ship Pro runtime
